title: Bulk report - Week 25 2016
summary:
Capesize
Some improvement in rates as the week closed out with more activity from Brazil although Vale largely in absentia. Rates here climbed to the low $9.00s but again for late July-August cargoes from Tubarao to Qingdao. A thinning in the list of ballasters heading to Brazil played a part in the rise.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: Some improvement in rates as the week closed out with more activity from Brazil although Vale largely in absentia. Rates here climbed to the low $9.00s but again for late July-August cargoes from Tubarao to Qingdao.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: Stronger rates in the north Atlantic with prompt tonnage tight. An 81,000 tonner fixed from the ARA for a trip via the Baltic to Skaw-Gibraltar at $6,000 daily - these ships a couple of weeks ago were seeing little more than $4,000 daily for quick rounds.

children.title: Supramax
children.summary: The market generally for supramax and handysizes ticked along at a steady pace with gains in the Asian sector with the US Gulf and east coast South America both having a rally. Period was on the agenda for a few charterers this week as a 66,000 dwt fixed delivery Oran for three to six months at $8,350 daily while another 63,000 dwt was suggested fixed for six to eight months delivery West Africa at $7,850 daily.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: US Gulf and east coast South America were the firmer areas for the smaller sizes. Small gains were made but with little definitive fixtures reported.
